info.title: GitRank API
info.description: Tracks GitHub activity of members across cohorts, ranks them on leaderboards, and awards titles (records + badges). Every joiner is also auto-added to the always-on `global` cohort, which uses a rolling 365-day window. Reads are public; `/admin/*` routes require a static bearer token.

tags.name: Cohorts
tags.description: Cohort listing, detail, and public self-serve join.

bearerAuth.description: Static admin token from the `ADMIN_TOKEN` env var.

kind.description: PROGRAM cohorts are time-boxed training cohorts. The single GLOBAL cohort (slug `global`) covers every joiner across a rolling 365-day window.

Snapshot.description: Latest stats for a member in a cohort; null if never synced. `xp` is denormalised from the other stat columns via `src/services/xp.js`. On the global cohort (rolling 365-day window) `xp` CAN decrease as old work falls out of the window — that is intentional. `calendar` is only present on the member profile response (for the heatmap); leaderboard rows omit it.

calendar.description: Daily contribution counts. Only present on the member profile response.

XpProgression.description: Per-cohort XP roll-up used to render the level ring in the UI.

levelProgress.description: [0, 1] — how far into the current level.

rankDelta.description: Change in rank vs. the immediately-previous snapshot set. Positive = climbed, negative = fell, 0 = unchanged. `null` for members without a previous snapshot.

holder.description: Current holder, or null if unclaimed.

member.description: Public member profile. `zid` is deliberately NOT included — it is PII and this endpoint is unauthenticated.

titles.description: Record titles held (including past/revoked), with their cohort.

history.description: Downsampled per-UTC-day snapshot rows in oldest-first order. Slim columns only — no calendar or topLanguages.

capturedAt.description: `null` when the member has no snapshot yet.

calendar.description: Daily contribution counts from the latest snapshot.

JoinRequest.description:
Strict: only `githubUsername` and `zid` are accepted. Any other field (including `displayName` and `programRepo`, which were removed) is rejected with a VALIDATION_ERROR. `displayName`/`avatarUrl` auto-populate from the verified GitHub profile; program repos are now organiser-managed via `PUT /admin/members/{username}/program-repo`.

**`zid` semantics vary by cohort:**
  - PROGRAM cohorts (any slug ≠ `global`): `zid` is **required**.
  - GLOBAL cohort (slug `global`): `zid` is **optional** — omit it (or send `""`) to join as a non-UNSW member. A member who joined without a zid can later "claim" one by joining a program cohort with the same GitHub username and a fresh zid — provided that zid isn't already linked to a different member.

zid.description: "z" followed by exactly 7 digits. Required for program cohorts, optional for `global`.

deleted.description: Number of ProgramRepo rows removed (0 if none registered).

UpdateCohortRequest.description: Patch a cohort. At least one field must be present. The `global` cohort accepts only `name`; any other field 403s. `kind` is not editable.

resyncTriggered.description: True when startDate/endDate changed — the sync + title evaluation for this cohort is running in the background so existing snapshots don't reflect a stale window.

titles.description: Always 0 — Title definitions are global, not cohort-scoped.

get.description:
Long-lived `text/event-stream` connection. Clients (browsers or `EventSource`) receive named events as soon as data changes. Heartbeat comments (`:hb`) are sent every 25 s to keep intermediate proxies from closing idle connections.

**Events emitted:**

| event             | payload |
| ----------------- | ------- |
| `sync.completed`  | `{ cohorts: [{ slug, snapshotsCreated }], finishedAt }` |
| `titles.changed`  | `{ slug, changes }` |
| `cohort.updated`  | `{ slug, previousSlug?, dateChanged }` |
| `cohort.deleted`  | `{ slug }` |

OpenAPI has no first-class SSE type — this endpoint is documented for discovery. The response body is a stream, not JSON.

200.description: The stream is open. Read frames as SSE.

get.description: Reports DB connectivity and the most recent snapshot time.

Default sort is `xp` — the primary progression metric. Ties break by `totalContributions` desc, then `accountCreatedAt` asc. Every entry carries a `rankDelta` field showing the change since the previous snapshot set (`null` if the member has no previous snapshot).

post.description: Verifies the GitHub user exists and adds them to the cohort. The body is strict — only `{ githubUsername, zid }` is accepted; any unknown field (including the removed `displayName` and `programRepo`) is rejected with a friendly `VALIDATION_ERROR`. `displayName`/`avatarUrl` are auto-populated from the verified GitHub profile (falling back to the login when the profile has no name). Program repos are now organiser-managed — see the admin endpoints. Reuses an existing member on an exact (zid, githubUsername) match; never silently re-links a zid to a different username.

400.description: Invalid body (bad zid format or an unexpected field)

403.description: Cohort is inactive or has ended

409.description: Duplicate zid/username belonging to a different identity

get.description: Latest stats per cohort, record titles held (incl. past), and badges.

get.summary: Time-series snapshots for a member in a cohort
get.description: Slim, chart-friendly view — one row per UTC calendar day (the last snapshot captured that day wins), oldest-first, within the last `days` days.

get.summary: Latest contribution-calendar for a member in a cohort
get.description: Returns the daily `{date, count}` calendar embedded in the member's most recent snapshot for the given cohort. Returns `capturedAt: null` and an empty calendar (200, not 404) when the member has no snapshot yet.

delete.description: Cascade-deletes memberships, snapshots and awards, then re-evaluates affected cohorts so vacated records transfer to the runner-up.

patch.description: Partial update — at least one editable field must be supplied. Date changes trigger a background re-sync + title re-evaluation for that cohort and set `resyncTriggered: true` on the response. The global cohort is protected: only `name` may be changed.

400.description: Empty body, invalid dates, or unknown field

403.description: Editing a protected field on the global cohort

delete.description: Cascade-deletes memberships, snapshots, and title awards for this cohort. Members themselves survive (they stay on `global` and any other cohorts). The global cohort cannot be deleted.

put.summary: Register a program repo for a membership
put.description: Organiser-managed: attaches a `ProgramRepo` to the member's membership in the given cohort. Replace-on-exists — one program repo per membership; any prior entries for that membership are removed. Night Owl and other program-repo-derived stats (e.g. the night-commit ratio) only activate for memberships with a registered repo, so cohorts without organiser-set repos simply cannot win Night Owl.

delete.summary: Remove a program repo from a membership

parameters.name: cohortSlug
parameters.description: The cohort slug whose membership the program repo belongs to.

post.summary: Trigger a manual sync + title evaluation
Run the sync + eval runner across every active cohort
post.description: External-cron trigger for free-tier hosts. Runs the same in-process runner as `node-cron` and shares its lock: if a sync is already running, returns `{ "skipped": true }` with 200 instead of double-syncing.

200.description: Runner summary (or `{ skipped: true }` if already running).
